channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description:
SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below -
CVE-2019-17571 (CVSS score: 9.8) - A code injection vulnerability in SAP Quotation Management Insurance application (FS-QUO) CVE-2026-27685 (CVSS score: 9.1) - An insecure deserialization
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Canada Needs Nationalized, Public AI
item.description: <p>Canada has a choice to make about its artificial intelligence future. The Carney administration is investing $2-billion over five years in its <a href="https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/ised/en/canadian-sovereign-ai-compute-strategy">Sovereign AI Compute Strategy</a>. Will any value generated by &#8220;sovereign AI&#8221; be captured in Canada, making a difference in the lives of Canadians, or is this just a passthrough to investment in American Big Tech?</p> <p>Forcing the question is OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, which has been pushing an &#8220;OpenAI for Countries&#8221; initiative. It is not the only one eyeing its share of the $2-billion, but it appears to be the most aggressive. OpenAI&#8217;s top lobbyist in the region has met with Ottawa officials, including Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cyber-Attacks on UK Firms Increase at Four Times Global Rate
item.description: Check Point data shows attack volumes are growing much faster in the UK than worldwide

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Two Publicly Disclosed Zero-Days
item.description: March Patch Tuesday sees Microsoft release updates for 79 flaws

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rated Critical, and 76 are rated Important in severity. Forty-six of the patched vulnerabilities relate to privilege escalation, followed by 18 remote code execution, 10 information disclosure, four
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours
item.description: A threat actor known as UNC6426 leveraged keys stolen following the supply chain compromise of the nx npm package last year to completely breach a victim's cloud environment within a span of 72 hours. The attack started with the theft of a developer's GitHub token, which the threat actor then used to gain unauthorized access to the cloud and steal data. "The threat actor, UNC6426, then used this
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Vendor Tiering Best Practices: Categorizing Vendor Risks | UpGuard
item.description: Vendor tiering is the key to a more efficient Vendor Risk Management program. Learn about its best practices.

item.title: What is Vendor Tiering? Optimize Your Vendor Risk Management | UpGuard
item.description: Learn what Vendor Tiering is and how it could improve the efficiency of your vendor risk management program.

item.title: Five Malicious Rust Crates and AI Bot Exploit CI/CD Pipelines to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered five malicious Rust crates that masquerade as time-related utilities to transmit .env file data to the threat actors. The Rust packages, published to crates.io, are listed below -
chrono_anchor dnp3times time_calibrator time_calibrators time-sync
The crates, per Socket, impersonate timeapi.io and were published between late February and early March
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Middle East Conflict Highlights Cloud Resilience Gaps
item.description: Data centers — used by both governments and militaries for operations — are now fair game, not just for cyberattacks, but for kinetic attacks as well.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Microsoft Patches 83 CVEs in March Update
item.description: For a change, there's little in this month's Patch Tuesday that should cause panic, according to security experts.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Hackers Pose as IT Staff in Microsoft Teams to Install Malware
item.description: <p>Hackers are impersonating IT staff in Microsoft Teams to trick employees into installing malware, giving attackers stealthy access to corporate networks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-teams-it-impersonation-malware-attack/">Hackers Pose as IT Staff in Microsoft Teams to Install Malware</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: 'Overly Permissive' Salesforce Cloud Configs in the Crosshairs
item.description: Some customers have mishandled guest user configurations otherwise intended to allow third-party access to important — and sensitive — client data.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description: After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Google’s $32B Wiz Acquisition Set to Become Israel’s Largest Tech Deal Ever
item.description: <p>Google’s $32 billion Wiz acquisition is nearing completion, marking a record Israeli tech exit and a major bet on cloud security.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/google-wiz-acquisition-32-billion-israel-tech-deal/">Google’s $32B Wiz Acquisition Set to Become Israel’s Largest Tech Deal Ever</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Madeline Clarke

item.title: Russian Hackers Target Signal and WhatsApp Accounts, Dutch Intelligence Warns
item.description: <p>Dutch intelligence says Russian state hackers are compromising Signal and WhatsApp accounts with phishing and linked-device tricks, underscoring how account security can fail even when encryption holds.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-russian-signal-whatsapp-hacked/">Russian Hackers Target Signal and WhatsApp Accounts, Dutch Intelligence Warns</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: OpenAI's Promptfoo Deal Plugs Agentic AI Testing Gap
item.description: OpenAI’s latest acquisition addresses a security need Jamieson O’Reilly, security advisor at OpenClaw, raised during an exclusive interview with Infosecurity

item.title: FortiGate Devices Exploited to Breach Networks and Steal Service Account Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks.&nbsp; The activity involves the exploitation of recently disclosed security vulnerabilities or weak credentials to extract configuration files containing service account credentials and network topology
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their own. But there is a problem. While these agents make work faster, they also open a new "back door" for hackers. The Problem: "The Invisible Employee" Think of an AI Agent like a new employee who has
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: KadNap Malware Infects 14,000+ Edge Devices to Power Stealth Proxy Botnet
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malware called KadNap that's primarily targeting Asus routers to enlist them into a botnet for proxying malicious traffic. The malware, first detected in the wild in August 2025, has expanded to over 14,000 infected devices, with more than 60% of victims located in the U.S., according to the Black Lotus Labs team at Lumen. A lesser number of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Only 24% Of organizations Test Identity Recovery Every Six Months
item.description: Only 24% of organizations test identity disaster recovery plans every 6 months, Quest Software said

item.title: Cloud Attackers Now Prefer Vulnerability Exploits Over Credentials, Google Cloud Finds
item.description: Google Cloud report details a sharp rise in attackers exploiting software vulnerabilities, including React2Shell

item.title: Fake Claude Code Spreads Malware to Windows, macOS Users
item.description: <p>Attackers are using fake Claude Code install pages and malicious search ads to spread infostealer malware targeting Windows and macOS systems.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-claude-code-install-pages-malware-windows-macos/">Fake Claude Code Spreads Malware to Windows, macOS Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Ericsson Breach Exposes Data of 15k Employees and Customers
item.description: Ericsson data breach affects 15k employees/customers after third-party service provider compromise

item.title: 'BlackSanta' EDR Killer Targets HR Workflows
item.description: A campaign by Russian-speaking cyberattackers hijacks workflows to deliver security-busting malware, allowing attackers to steal data without detection.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and exfiltrate sensitive data within organizations' Google Cloud environments. The shortcomings have been collectively named LeakyLooker by Tenable. There is no evidence that the vulnerabilities were exploited in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
item.description: You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing exposure than they realise. Intruder's Head of Security digs into why this happens and how teams can manage it deliberately. Time-to-exploit is shrinking The larger and less controlled your attack surface is,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: APT28 Uses BEARDSHELL and COVENANT Malware to Spy on Ukrainian Military
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group tracked as APT28 has been observed using a pair of implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long‑term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel. The two malware families have been put to use since April 2024, ESET said in a new report shared with The Hacker News. APT28, also tracked as Blue Athena, BlueDelta, Fancy Bear, Fighting Ursa,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russian Hackers Target WhatsApp and Signal Accounts of Global Military and Government Officials
item.description: Dutch intelligence reveals Russian state hackers are trying to hijack the Signal and WhatsApp accounts of key targets

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Hundreds of Websites in New Salesforce Campaign
item.description: Prolific ShinyHunters group claims to have stolen data from nearly 400 websites in Experience Cloud attacks

item.title: Sednit reloaded: Back in the trenches
item.description: The resurgence of one of Russia’s most notorious APT groups

item.title: Jailbreaking the F-35 Fighter Jet
item.description: <p>Countries around the world are becoming increasingly concerned about their dependencies on the US. If you&#8217;ve purchase US-made F-35 fighter jets, you are dependent on the US for software maintenance.</p> <p>The Dutch Defense Secretary recently <a href="https://www.twz.com/air/f-35-software-could-be-jailbreaked-like-an-iphone-dutch-defense-minister">said</a> that he could jailbreak the planes to accept third-party software.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an open-source tool called AuraInspector. The activity, per the company, involves the exploitation of customers' overly permissive Experience Cloud guest user configurations to obtain access to sensitive
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability list is as follows -
CVE-2021-22054 (CVSS score: 7.5) - A server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Omnissa Workspace One UEM (formerly VMware Workspace One UEM) that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: White House Cyber Strategy Prioritizes Offense
item.description: In a seven-page strategy document, the Trump administration signaled a shift to preemption and deterrence to handling cyber threats.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: 'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
item.description: A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Are We Ready for Auto Remediation With Agentic AI?
item.description: With the rapid innovations in AI, we are entering an exciting era of automated risk remediation. Learn about security team readiness to leverage agentic AI for threat and exposure management.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Marks

item.title: Welcome Our Newest Associate Participating Organizations
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Veeam’s ‘Agent Commander’: Bringing Guardrails and Resilience to the Wild West of AI
item.description: <p>Veeam’s Agent Commander turns backup into an AI-era command center, giving enterprises the guardrails, visibility, and precision “undo” they need to safely scale autonomous agents.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-veeam-agent-commander-ai-resilience/">Veeam’s ‘Agent Commander’: Bringing Guardrails and Resilience to the Wild West of AI</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Zeus Kerravala

item.title: Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The package, named "@openclaw-ai/openclawai," was uploaded to the registry by a user named "openclaw-ai" on March 3, 2026. It has been downloaded 178 times to date. The library is still available for
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Threat Actor Exploits Flaws and Uses Elastic Cloud SIEM to Manage Stolen Data
item.description: Huntress researchers uncover campaign exploiting vulnerabilities to steal data using Elastic Cloud as a data hub

item.title: Trump Administration Unveils New Cyber Strategy for America
item.description: US national cyber strategy focuses on stronger defenses, countering threats, fostering innovation

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The activity has been attributed with moderate confidence to the state-sponsored adversary, which is also tracked under the cryptonyms Jade Sleet, PUKCHONG, Slow Pisces, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Launches New Crackdown Unit to Tackle Cyber-Fraud at the Source
item.description: New UK Online Crime Centre will combine expertise from a range of sources to takedown online channels cyber-scammers rely on

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Security Startups Dominate New Cyber Innovation Awards
item.description: Over one in five winners of IT-Harvest’s 2026 Cyber 150 are AI security companies

item.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
item.description: An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partners are defining the security level you must meet. What if you could be the enabler for your organization to remain competitive — and help win business — by easily demonstrating that you meet these
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Attack Against Wi-Fi
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: TriZetto Provider Solutions Breach Hits 3.4 Million Patients
item.description: Billing services provider TriZetto Provider Solutions has begun notifying millions of patients about a data breach

item.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
item.description:
Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code, and harvest sensitive data. The extensions in question, both originally associated with a developer named "akshayanuonline@gmail.com" (BuildMelon), are listed below -
QuickLens - Search Screen with
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ghanaian Pleads Guilty to Role in $100m Romance Scam
item.description: Derrick Van Yeboah admitted he stole over $10m in romance scams as part of crime gang

item.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
item.description: High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign. The activity, which has targeted aviation, energy, government, law enforcement, pharmaceutical, technology, and telecommunications sectors, has been attributed by Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 to a previously undocumented threat activity group dubbed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Vendor Tiering Series: Mapping Tiers to Inherent Risk | UpGuard
item.description: Subjective labels do not categorize vendor risk. Learn how to build a defensible tiering architecture using objective criteria and a scalable model.

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is available in a research preview to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, Business, and Edu customers via the Codex web with free usage for the next month. "It builds deep context about your project to identify
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic Finds 22 Firefox Vulnerabilities Using Claude Opus 4.6 AI Model
item.description: Anthropic on Friday said it discovered 22 new security vulnerabilities in the Firefox web browser as part of a security partnership with Mozilla. Of these, 14 have been classified as high, seven have been classified as moderate, and one has been rated low in severity. The issues were addressed in Firefox 148, released late last month. The vulnerabilities were identified over a two-week period in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Investigates Suspicious Activity in Surveillance Platform
item.description: <p>The FBI is investigating suspicious cyber activity in a system used to process surveillance and wiretap warrants, raising concerns about security risks to sensitive data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fbi-investigates-cyber-activity-surveillance-warrant-system/">FBI Investigates Suspicious Activity in Surveillance Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid in Byzantine Monk Cooking
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cylake Offers AI-Native Security Without Relying on Cloud Services
item.description: Cylake's platform will analyze security data locally and identify potential attacks for organizations concerned about data sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: North Korean APTs Use AI to Enhance IT Worker Scams
item.description: DPRK worker scams are old hat, but they're still working, thanks to AI tools that help with everything from face swapping to daily emails.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: <p><a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/27/technology/openai-agreement-pentagon-ai.html">OpenAI is in</a> and <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/28/openai-us-military-anthropic">Anthropic is out</a> as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the wealthiest titans of the big tech industry, and the overhanging specter of the existential risks posed by a new technology powerful enough that the Pentagon claims it is essential to national security. At issue is Anthropic&#8217;s <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-department-of-war">insistence</a> that the US Department of Defense (DoD) could not use its models to facilitate &#8220;mass surveillance&#8221; or &#8220;fully autonomous weapons,&#8221; provisions the defense secretary Pete Hegseth ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: LexisNexis Hack Exposes 3.9M Records Through Unpatched React Vulnerability
item.description: <p>LexisNexis confirmed a data breach after hackers leaked stolen files, with attackers claiming they exploited the React2Shell vulnerability.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-lexisnexis-breach-3-9m-records-react-vulnerability/">LexisNexis Hack Exposes 3.9M Records Through Unpatched React Vulnerability</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Iran's MuddyWater Hackers Hit US Firms with New 'Dindoor' Backdoor
item.description: A bank, an airport, a non-profit and the Israeli branch of a US software company were among the targets of this new MuddyWater campaign

item.title: Transparent Tribe Uses AI to Mass-Produce Malware Implants in Campaign Targeting India
item.description: The Pakistan-aligned threat actor known as Transparent Tribe has become the latest hacking group to embrace artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding tools to strike targets with various implants. The activity is designed to produce a "high-volume, mediocre mass of implants" that are developed using lesser-known programming languages like Nim, Zig, and Crystal and rely on trusted services like
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: EU Auto Rules Shift Gears on Cybersecurity Standards
item.description: The European Union is taking new precautions as climate change and cybersecurity threats rise across the automotive industry.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 2–6.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Bitwarden Brings Passkey Logins to Windows 11, Expanding Passwordless Sign-Ins
item.description: <p>Bitwarden now supports passkey logins on Windows 11 for Microsoft Entra ID users, extending passwordless sign-ins to device authentication.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-bitwarden-passkey-windows-11-sign-in/">Bitwarden Brings Passkey Logins to Windows 11, Expanding Passwordless Sign-Ins</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Multi-Stage VOID#GEIST Malware Delivering XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a multi-stage malware campaign that uses batch scripts as a pathway to deliver various encrypted remote access trojan (RATs) payloads that correspond to XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT. The stealthy attack chain has been codenamed VOID#GEIST by Securonix Threat Research. At a high level, the obfuscated batch script is used to deploy a second
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran's Cyber-Kinetic War Doctrine Takes Shape
item.description: Iran has been hacking IP cameras to plan missile strikes against its enemies, and mounting other attacks on physical assets, showing how cyber and kinetic warfare are fast becoming one and the same.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Cyberattack on Mexico's Gov't Agencies Highlight AI Threat
item.description: Using Anthropic's Claude, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and a detailed playbook prompt, a handful of cyberattackers reportedly gained access to government agencies and its citizens' data.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Zero‑Day Attacks on Enterprise Software Reach Record High, Google Warns
item.description: Almost a quarter of the zero days detected by Google in 2025 targeted security and networking appliances

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that model. When done right, it builds client trust, increases upsell opportunities, and drives recurring revenue. But to deliver this consistently and efficiently, you need the right technology and processes.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Hackers Target U.S. Networks With New Dindoor Backdoor
item.description: New research from Broadcom's Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter Team has discovered evidence of an Iranian hacking group embedding itself in several U.S. companies' networks, including banks, airports, non-profit, and the Israeli arm of a software company. The activity has been attributed to a state-sponsored hacking group called MuddyWater (aka Seedworm). It's affiliated with the Iranian
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What cybersecurity actually does for your business
item.description: The ability to continue operating safely in an unsafe environment where competitors cannot is a competitive advantage that is rarely measured or discussed

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Use TernDoor, PeerTime, BruteEntry in South American Telecom Attacks
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure in South America since 2024, targeting Windows and Linux systems and edge devices with three different implants. The activity is being tracked by Cisco Talos under the moniker UAT-9244, describing it as closely associated with another cluster known as FamousSparrow. It's worth
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and deploy the Lumma Stealer malware. The activity, observed in February 2026, makes use of the terminal emulator program instead of instructing users to launch the Windows Run dialog and paste a command
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The critical-severity vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2017-7921 (CVSS score: 9.8) - An improper authentication vulnerability affecting
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Hackers Used New Exploit Kit to Compromise Thousands of iPhones
item.description: <p>Thousands of iPhones were compromised using the Coruna exploit kit, which chained 23 iOS vulnerabilities into advanced attacks used for espionage and cybercrime.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-coruna-exploit-kit-thousands-of-iphones-compromised/">Hackers Used New Exploit Kit to Compromise Thousands of iPhones</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Cisco Drops 48 New Firewall Vulnerabilities, 2 Critical
item.description: Edge bugs are so fetch, and Cisco just patched 50 new ones, including some heavy hitters with 10 out of 10 scores on the CVSS scale.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: How to Comply with HECVAT in 2026 (Free Checklist) | UpGuard
item.description: If you're a vendor supporting the education sector, this checklist will help you prepare for any HECVAT questionnaires that come your way.

item.title: Israel Hacked Traffic Cameras in Iran
item.description: <p><a href="https://www.timesofisrael.com/report-israel-hacked-tehran-traffic-cameras-to-track-khamenei-ahead-of-assassination/">Multiple</a> <a href="https://www.ft.com/content/bf998c69-ab46-4fa3-aae4-8f18f7387836">news</a> <a href="https://www.channelnewsasia.com/world/iran-war-inside-plan-kill-ali-khamenei-5966861">outlets</a> are reporting on Israel&#8217;s hacking of Iranian traffic cameras and how they assisted with the killing of that country&#8217;s leadership.</p> <p><i>The New York Times</i> has an &#60;a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/01/us/politics/cia-israel-ayatollah-compound.html"&#60;article</a> on the intelligence operation more generally.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Fig Security Emerges From Stealth to Fix Broken Security Operations
item.description: Fig Security's platform traces security data flows end-to-end across SIEMs, pipelines, and response systems to alert teams before infrastructure changes break critical defenses.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Software Development Practices Help Enterprises Tackle Real-Life Risks
item.description: Organizations can borrow secure-by-design processes to manage non-technical challenges like governance or the inevitable human error.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: AI-Driven Insider Risk Now a “Critical Business Threat,” Report Warns
item.description: Malicious insiders are using misusing AI for nefarious gain, while employees cutting corners also creates risk, warns Mimecast

item.title: Cisco Confirms Active Exploitation of Two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Cisco has disclosed that two more vulnerabilities affecting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly SD-WAN vManage) have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below -
CVE-2026-20122 (CVSS score: 7.1) - An arbitrary file overwrite vulnerability that could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to overwrite arbitrary files on the local file system.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Preparing for the Quantum Era: Post-Quantum Cryptography Webinar for Security Leaders
item.description: Most organizations assume encrypted data is safe. But many attackers are already preparing for a future where today’s encryption can be broken. Instead of trying to decrypt information now, they are collecting encrypted data and storing it so it can be decrypted later using quantum computers. This tactic—known as “harvest now, decrypt later”—means sensitive data transmitted today could become
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: ContextCrush Flaw Exposes AI Development Tools to Attacks
item.description: Critical flaw "ContextCrush" in Context7 MCP Server could allow malicious instructions into AI tools

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: DDR5 Bot Scalping, Samsung TV Tracking, Reddit Privacy Fine & More
item.description: Some weeks in cybersecurity feel routine. This one doesn&rsquo;t. Several new developments surfaced over the past few days, showing how quickly the threat landscape keeps shifting. Researchers uncovered fresh activity, security teams shared new findings, and a few unexpected moves from major tech companies also drew attention. Together, these updates offer a useful snapshot of what is happening
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coruna Exploit Kit Targets Older iPhones in Multi-Stage Campaigns
item.description: Exploit kit "Coruna" targets iPhones running iOS 13.0 to 17.2.1, focusing on financial data theft

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before-seen malware. Zscaler ThreatLabz, which observed the activity in January 2026, is tracking the cluster under the name Dust Specter. The attacks, which manifest in the form of two different
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacked App Part of US/Israeli Propaganda Campaign Against Iran
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attackers still compromise networks every day using valid credentials. The issue is not MFA itself, but coverage.&nbsp; Enforced through an identity provider (IdP) such as Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zero-Click FreeScout Bug Enables Remote Code Execution
item.description: Ox Security warns that Mail2Shell could enable threat actors to hijack FreeScout systems without user interaction
